items.title: Anthropic wins injunction against Trump administration over Defense Department saga
items.summary: A federal judge has ordered that the Trump administration rescind recent restrictions it placed on the AI company.

items.title: You can now transfer your chats and personal information from other chatbots directly into Gemini
items.summary: Google is launching "switching tools" that, just as it sounds, will make it easier for users of other chatbots to switch to Gemini.

items.title: Wikipedia cracks down on the use of AI in article writing
items.summary: The site, whose policies are subject to change, has struggled with the issue of AI-generated writing.

items.title: OpenAI abandons yet another side quest: ChatGPT’s erotic mode
items.summary: It's only the latest of several side projects that the AI startup has ditched over the past week.

items.title: Data centers get ready — the Senate wants to see your power bills
items.summary: Senators Josh Hawley and Elizabeth Warren want the Energy Information Administration to gather more details about how data centers use power — and how that affects the grid.

items.title: ByteDance’s new AI video generation model, Dreamina Seedance 2.0, comes to CapCut
items.summary: The new model in CapCut will have built-in protections for making video from real faces or unauthorized intellectual property.

items.title: Conntour raises $7M from General Catalyst, YC to build an AI search engine for security video systems
items.summary: Conntour uses AI models to let security teams query camera feeds using natural language to find any object, person, or situation.

items.title: Cohere launches an open source voice model specifically for transcription
items.summary: Relatively light at just 2 billion parameters, the model is meant for use with consumer-grade GPUs for those who want to self-host it. It currently supports 14 languages.

items.title: A ‘pound of flesh’ from data centers: one senator’s answer to AI job losses
items.summary: Fears of AI-driven job loss are growing fast, and they’re fueling backlash against data centers. Sen. Mark Warner suggests taxing them to help workers survive the transition.

items.title: Mistral releases a new open source model for speech generation
items.summary: The model, which lets enterprises build voice agents for sales and customer engagement, puts Mistral in direct competition with the likes of ElevenLabs, Deepgram, and OpenAI.

items.title: The least surprising chapter of the Manus story is what’s happening right now
items.summary: Did anyone think there would not be a reckoning over this tie-up?

items.title: Mercor competitor Deccan AI raises $25M, sources experts from India
items.summary: Deccan AI concentrates its workforce in India to manage quality in a fast-growing but fragmented AI training market.

items.title: The AI skills gap is here, says AI company, and power users are pulling ahead
items.summary: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.

items.title: Google unveils TurboQuant, a new AI memory compression algorithm — and yes, the internet is calling it ‘Pied Piper’
items.summary: Google’s TurboQuant has the internet joking about Pied Piper from HBO's "Silicon Valley." The compression algorithm promises to shrink AI’s “working memory” by up to 6x, but it’s still just a lab experiment for now.

items.title: Melania Trump wants a robot to homeschool your child
items.summary: The first lady sees AI and robotics playing a prominent role in the future of American education.

items.title: Bernie Sanders and AOC propose a ban on data center construction
items.summary: Senator Bernie Sanders and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduced companion legislation to halt construction on new data centers until Congress passes comprehensive AI regulation.

items.title: Google launches Lyria 3 Pro music generation model
items.summary: Google is launching Lyria 3 Pro, an upgraded music model that generates longer, more customizable tracks, as it expands AI music tools across Gemini, enterprise products, and other services.

items.title: Reddit takes on the bots with new ‘human verification’ requirements for fishy behavior
items.summary: Reddit will require suspected automated accounts to verify they’re human, as it ramps up efforts to curb bot-driven spam and manipulation.

items.title: Harvey confirms $11B valuation: Sequoia triples down
items.summary: Investors like Sequoia, Andreessen Horowitz,  Kleiner Perkins, and Elad Gil can't get enough of AI legal tech startup Harvey.

items.title: Granola raises $125M, hits $1.5B valuation as it expands from meeting notetaker to enterprise AI app
items.summary: Granola's valuation jumped from $250 million to $1.5 billion with this round, and it has added more support for AI agents after users previously complained.

items.title: Judge sides with Anthropic to temporarily block the Pentagon’s ban
items.summary: After Anthropic's weeks-long standoff with the Pentagon, the company won one milestone: A judge granted Anthropic a preliminary injunction in its lawsuit, which sought to reverse its government blacklisting while the judicial process plays out. "The Department of War's records show that it designated Anthropic as a supply chain risk because of its 'hostile manner […]

items.title: David Sacks is no longer the White House AI and Crypto Czar
items.summary: David Sacks, the venture capitalist and tech billionaire who'd become Silicon Valley's primary advocate inside the White House and a key architect of its aggressive AI policy initiatives, revealed on Thursday that he was no longer a special government employee - and therefore no longer President Donald Trump's Special Advisor on AI and Crypto. Sacks' […]

items.title: Google is making it easier to import another AI’s memory into Gemini
items.summary: After Anthropic updated its tool for copying another AI's memory into Claude earlier this month, Google Gemini is rolling out new "Import Memory" and "Import Chat History" features on desktop that can help users quickly copy over everything their current AI already knows about them. To use the "Import Memory" tool, users copy and paste […]

items.title: Apple will reportedly allow other AI chatbots to plug into Siri
items.summary: Apple's iOS 27 update will allow users to choose the AI chatbot they want to link with Siri. That's according to a report from Bloomberg's Mark Gurman, who says third-party chatbots downloaded from the App Store, like Google's Gemini or Anthropic's Claude, will be able to fetch replies for Siri - similar to how the […]

items.title: Apple’s AI Playlist Playground is bad at music
items.summary: Apple Music: "What do you want to hear?" Me: "Atmospheric instrumental black metal to write to." Apple Music: "Here's three metal songs with vocals, a field recording, an ambient electronic track, and a piece of doom jazz." I am skeptical of AI's ability to serve up the music I want to begin with, but even […]

items.title: Google’s ‘live’ AI search assistant can handle conversations in dozens more languages
items.summary: Google is expanding access to Search Live, a feature that lets you search for information using your voice and camera. The AI search assistant is now available in more than 200 countries and territories, as well as dozens of languages, according to an announcement on Thursday. Search Live rolled out broadly in the US last […]

items.title: Meta gets ready to launch two new Ray-Ban AI glasses
items.summary: This is Lowpass by Janko Roettgers, a newsletter on the ever-evolving intersection of tech and entertainment, syndicated just for The Verge subscribers once a week. Meta and its AI glasses hardware partner EssilorLuxottica are getting ready to launch the next generation of their Ray-Ban AI glasses. That's according to a series of FCC filings for […]

items.title: Wikipedia bans AI-generated articles
items.summary: Wikipedia will no longer allow editors to write or rewrite articles using AI. The update, which was added to Wikipedia's guidelines late last week, cites the tendency for AI-written articles to violate "several of Wikipedia's core content policies" as the reason for the ban. The change applies to the English version of Wikipedia and will […]

items.title: Senators are pushing to find out how much electricity data centers actually use
items.summary: On Thursday, senators Elizabeth Warren (D-MA) and Josh Hawley (R-MO) sent a letter to the Energy Information Administration (EIA) asking it to collect "comprehensive, annual energy-use disclosures" on data centers and make that information publicly available, as first reported by Wired. They're urging the agency to "establish a mandatory annual reporting requirement for data centers," […]

items.title: Webtoon is adding AI localization tools to its comics platform
items.summary: Canvas, Webtoon's platform for user-uploaded comics, is about to get a major overhaul that's designed to help creators make more money and share their art with a wider audience. Today, Webtoon announced its plans to roll out a number of new features for Canvas that will make it easier for artists to build global followings […]

items.title: The snow gods: How a couple of ski bums built the internet’s best weather app
items.summary: The best snow-forecasting app for skiers and snowboarders isn’t from any of the federally funded weather services. Nor from any of the big-name brands. It’s an independent app startup that leverages government data, its own AI models, and decades of alpine-life experience to offer better snow (and soon avalanche) predictions than anything else out there.…

items.title: Are high gas prices good news for EVs? It’s complicated.
items.summary: I live in a dense city with plentiful public transportation options and limited parking, so I don’t own a car. I’m often utterly clueless about the current price of gasoline. But as the conflict in Iran has escalated, fossil-fuel prices have been on a roller-coaster, and I’ve started paying attention. In the US, average gas…

items.title: Roundtables: The Next Era of Space Exploration
items.summary: Listen to the session or watch below Whether it’s the race to find life on Mars, the campaign to outsmart killer asteroids, or the quest to make the moon a permanent home to astronauts, scientists’ efforts in space can tell us more about where humanity is headed. This subscriber-only discussion examines the progress and possibilities…

items.title: Why this battery company is pivoting to AI
items.summary: Qichao Hu doesn’t mince words about how he sees the state of the battery industry. “Almost every Western battery company has either died or is going to die. It’s kind of the reality,” he says. Hu is the CEO of SES AI, a Massachusetts-based battery company. It once had aims of making huge amounts of…

items.title: This startup wants to change how mathematicians do math
items.summary: Axiom Math, a startup based in Palo Alto, California, has released a free new AI tool for mathematicians, designed to discover mathematical patterns that could unlock solutions to long-standing problems. The tool, called Axplorer, is a redesign of an existing one called PatternBoost that François Charton, now a research scientist at Axiom, co-developed in 2024…

items.title: The Download: reawakening frozen brains, and the AI Hype Index returns
items.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. This scientist rewarmed and studied pieces of his friend’s cryopreserved brain  L. Stephen Coles’s brain sits in a vat at a storage facility in Arizona. It has been held there at a temperature…

items.title: Agentic commerce runs on truth and context
items.summary: Imagine telling a digital agent, “Use my points and book a family trip to Italy. Keep it within budget, pick hotels we’ve liked before, and handle the details.” Instead of returning a list of links, the agent assembles an itinerary and executes the purchase. That shift, from assistance to execution, is what makes agentic AI…

items.title: The AI Hype Index: AI goes to war
items.summary: AI is at war. Anthropic and the Pentagon feuded over how to weaponize Anthropic’s AI model Claude; then OpenAI swept the Pentagon off its feet with an “opportunistic and sloppy” deal. Users quit ChatGPT in droves. People marched through London in the biggest protest against AI to date. If you’re keeping score, Anthropic—the company founded…

items.title: Exclusive eBook: Are we ready to hand AI agents the keys?
items.summary: We’re starting to give AI agents real autonomy, but are we prepared for what could happen next? This subscriber-only eBook explores this and angles from experts, such as “If we continue on the current path … we are basically playing Russian roulette with humanity.” by Grace Huckins June 12, 2025 Related Stories: Access all subscriber-only…
